plan_metadata.name: Sample Tab With Azure Backend Remote Debug

steps.description: Type 'tab app with azure backend' into the Samples search/filter input box in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit pane of Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Create" button next to "Tab App with Azure Back..." under the Samples section in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel in Visual Studio Code.
And input ${{var:app_name:tab-with-backend}} into the run time variable.
Use the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P in Visual Studio Code to activate the Command Palette, enabling quick access to commands within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace.
Type the text "deploy" into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field to initiate the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Deploy" command within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
Press the Enter key to select the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Deploy" command in the VS Code command palette after searching for ">deploy".
Click the "Deploy" button in the confirmation dialog asking, "Do you want to deploy resources in dev environment?" within the Visual Studio Code interface.

Click the "View provisioned resources" button in the notification popup within the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface, specifically in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit context. This button confirms the successful completion of the provisioning stage and provides access to the deployed resources.
Press the Enter key in the Visual Studio Code integrated terminal to confirm installation of "@azure/static-web-apps-cli" after the prompt "Ok to proceed? (y/n)".
Click the illustrated "Congratulations!" banner in the center of the Teams tab interface within a Chrome browser window.
Press the End key to scroll to the bottom of the "tab-with-backendlocal" content pane in the Microsoft Teams web interface displayed in Google Chrome.
Click the "Authorize and Call Azure Function" blue button in the tab-with-backendlocal section of the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Accept" button on the Microsoft account permissions request dialog to grant requested app permissions.
@assertion there's message: "User display name is " exist.
